diff_quality.notes: Clean, idiomatic Astro-islands integration. Chat UI is browser-only (client:only="react") which is correct because the UI Kit touches window/document at import. Auth is a proper token flow: no CometChat credentials in the client bundle; browser fetches same-origin /api/cometchat/session which proxies to backend /cometchat/token using the httpOnly session cookie, then loginWithAuthToken. REST API key stays in backend env (COMETCHAT_REST_API_KEY). Both 1:1 (dispatcher-hub) and group (route + exception) conversations built with chat + voice/video. RBAC is server-derived (membership provisioned server-side, not client-filtered). StrictMode double-mount guarded via module-level init/login flags. Call buttons correctly left to CometChatMessageHeader auto-render (no double-render). Only smell: `region as 'us'|'eu'|'in'` cast, forced by narrow SDK typing; and defensive optional-chaining on endSession/leaveSession to bridge v4/v5 naming.

queries.query: React UI Kit call events ccCallEnded CometChatCallEvents subscribe end session clear active call

coverage_gaps: No calls/react-calls bundle exists in docs-MCP — only react-uikit-quickstart. The agent had to fall back to the cometchat-react-calls SKILL file for CometChatCalls.init order and call-teardown event wiring; docs-MCP contributed only the events page.
docs-MCP gave nothing useful on the endSession vs leaveSession naming difference across Calls SDK v4/v5; the agent had to access both defensively via optional chaining.
staleness_conflicts: The react-uikit-quickstart bundle still documents `CometChatConversationsWithMessages`, which no longer exists in UI Kit v6. The agent detected this (via the react-calls skill note) and hand-composed CometChatConversations + CometChatMessageHeader/List/Composer instead — but the stale doc would mislead an agent that trusted it.

blockers.issue: 0 screenshots captured — web flow driver lacks captureShot (update the use case's _driver.mjs) (/Users/admin/Desktop/orchestrator/cometchat-skills-reviewer/cometchat-skills-reviewer/_reports/2026-07-08-baseline/logistics/web/smoke)

docs-MCP react-uikit-quickstart bundle is stale for v6: it still shows the removed CometChatConversationsWithMessages component, forcing the agent to discover the composed Conversations + MessageHeader/List/Composer shape from the skill file instead of docs.

No calling bundle in docs-MCP: init order (separate CometChatCalls.init after CometChatUIKit.init), incoming-call mounting, and callee-side teardown all had to come from the cometchat-react-calls skill; docs-MCP only covered the call-events symbol page.

Calls SDK types `region` as a narrow union ('us'|'eu'|'in') while the backend supplies a runtime-validated string, forcing a type assertion at CometChatCalls.init — a minor SDK ergonomics gap that pushes an unsafe cast into integration code.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Update the docs-MCP react-uikit-quickstart bundle to v6: remove CometChatConversationsWithMessages and show the composed inbox+thread shape (CometChatConversations + CometChatMessageHeader/MessageList/MessageComposer) with the onItemClick → getConversationWith() selection pattern.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: docs-MCP react-uikit-quickstart bundle is stale for v6 (CometChatConversationsWithMessages removed).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a dedicated react-calls doc bundle to docs-MCP covering the dual-SDK init order, <CometChatIncomingCall /> app-wide mounting, and the ccCallEnded/ccCallRejected teardown + clearActiveCall/endSession/leaveSession release path, so calling integration doesn't depend solely on the skill file.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: No calling bundle in docs-MCP; agent relied entirely on the cometchat-react-calls skill for init order and teardown.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In the cometchat-react-calls skill, document the region-typing union and the v4/v5 endSession-vs-leaveSession naming split, with the recommended validated-string cast and optional-chaining pattern, so integrators don't have to rediscover the workaround.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Calls SDK narrow region union forces an unsafe cast and session-teardown method name differs across v4/v5.

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

- **Skill variants relied on:** `cometchat-astro-patterns` (React islands, `client:only`, CSS-inside-island, `PUBLIC_` env), `cometchat-react-calls` (dual-SDK init order, additive ringing, don't-double-call-buttons, `CometChatCalls.init`), plus `cometchat` dispatcher and the react-uikit-quickstart bundle. Docs-MCP used for the React v6 events page (call-event symbols). Ground truth: `@cometchat/chat-uikit-react@^6` + `@cometchat/chat-sdk-javascript@^4` + `@cometchat/calls-sdk-javascript@^5`.
- **Where skills/docs were wrong/outdated:** The `react-uikit-quickstart` bundle still shows `CometChatConversationsWithMessages`, which **no longer exists in v6** (the react-calls skill explicitly notes it was removed). I used the composed `Conversations` + `MessageHeader/List/Composer` shape instead. No `react-calls` bundle exists in docs-MCP (only `react-uikit-quickstart`); I used the `cometchat-react-calls` SKILL file for calls.
- **Candidate hallucinations (verified before use):** `CometChatCallEvents.ccCallEnded/ccCallRejected` — verified against the React v6 events doc page. `CometChat.clearActiveCall()`, `CometChatCalls.init({appId,region})`, `loginWithAuthToken` — all resolved cleanly at type-check + build, so the symbols exist in the installed packages. `CometChatCalls.endSession/leaveSession` accessed defensively (optional-chained) since the name differs across v4/v5.
- **Docs-escapes (web-search/guess):** None. I stayed within docs-MCP + the skill files. The only cast is `region as 'us'|'eu'|'in'` because the Calls SDK types region as a narrow union while the backend supplies a validated string.
- **Scope completed:** BOTH 1:1 (direct user-to-user) AND group conversations built, each with chat + voice **and** video. **Chats tab is on the dashboard** primary nav (nav item label: **"Chats"**, `/chats`) for every role. RBAC respected (CometChat user carries role; membership is server-derived; dispatcher-hub enforced). Nothing left incomplete for the web scope. (Backend token-mint/user-provisioning already existed in `../backend` and was consumed read-only, not modified, per component-scope rules.)
- **call-teardown:** I **created** an explicit callee-side teardown frame — `src/components/cometchat/CallTeardown.tsx`. It subscribes to `CometChatCallEvents.ccCallEnded` and `ccCallRejected` (kit events) plus `CometChat.addCallListener`'s `onIncomingCallCancelled`/`onOutgoingCallRejected` (Chat-SDK safety net), and on any of them runs `releaseActiveCall()`: `CometChat.clearActiveCall()` (dismisses the ongoing/incoming view on the **callee/non-initiator** side) + `CometChatCalls.leaveSession()`/`endSession()` (releases the local WebRTC session so a subsequent call connects rather than returning "busy"). `<CometChatIncomingCall />` is mounted here app-wide so it survives navigation. Verified structurally: the listeners are registered at island mount (above the route/thread), fire regardless of which conversation is open, and are cleaned up on unmount (`unsubscribe()` / `removeCallListener`). Runtime two-user call verification requires a live browser + two sessions, which isn't available in this build-only gate; the teardown path is wired to the exact end/reject/cancel events for that scenario.
